title: quercus suber f. dolichocarpa
intro: Quercus suber f. dolichocarpa, also known as the Cork Oak, is a variant of the Quercus suber plant, native to the Mediterranean region and valued for its unique properties. The bark of Quercus suber f. dolichocarpa contains tannins and other compounds that have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Studies have shown that the plant's extracts possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, protecting against infections, and supporting overall well-being. Quercus suber f. dolichocarpa can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and promote health.
